handoffNotes: Assigned feature: 00-preflight.
Coding model used: GPT-5.5 High.

Implemented only preflight engineering foundation: package manifest, framework configuration, strict TypeScript, Tailwind styling, shadcn/ui-compatible primitives, minimal smoke shell, unit smoke test, and Playwright smoke test.

Fixed verification dependency issues by pinning the project to Node 24/npm 11, removing obsolete local Node v23.3.0, upgrading direct dependencies to current compatible releases, and regenerating package-lock.json.

Resolved Vitest/Vite audit findings with vitest@4.1.9 and vite@8.0.16; resolved jsdom/canvas deprecated chain with jsdom@29.1.1 and react-pdf@10.4.1/PDF.js updates; resolved Next internal PostCSS audit by overriding Next's postcss dependency to ^8.5.15.
Updated Tailwind/PostCSS config for Tailwind CSS 4 and updated Next/Playwright configuration so production build and E2E smoke tests run without HMR console noise.

The visible UI is intentionally a placeholder for install/build/browser smoke checks and states that product modules are unavailable; no metronome, recording, sheet, reference, settings, or v1 behavior was implemented.
Implementation commit hash is reported in the final handoff response because a git commit cannot contain its own final object hash without changing that hash.
changedAreas: package.json scripts and dependency declarations for Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind, shadcn-compatible primitives, lucide-react, Zustand, Zod, Dexie, Tone.js, wavesurfer.js, react-pdf/PDF.js, Vitest, and Playwright
Next.js app scaffold under src/app with a minimal preflight shell page
shadcn/ui-compatible utility and primitive components under src/components/ui and src/lib
Architecture boundary placeholders under src/domain, src/services, src/infrastructure, src/stores, src/hooks, src/test/helpers, and test-fixtures
Vitest smoke unit test under tests/unit
Playwright smoke e2e test under tests/e2e covering render, desktop/mobile resize, and console error capture
Tooling config files: tsconfig, next config, Tailwind/PostCSS, ESLint, Prettier, Vitest, Playwright, .gitignore, .npmrc, .nvmrc, and README setup notes
knownRisks: The project now requires Node >=24.0.0 and npm >=11.17.0 with engine-strict=true; Node 23 is intentionally unsupported because current Vitest/Vite/jsdom toolchain releases do not support that odd Node line.
Playwright reports this OS is not officially supported and uses its ubuntu24.04 fallback Chromium build; chromium install and E2E both completed successfully in this environment.
Next.js 16/Turbopack build required unsandboxed execution here because sandboxed execution blocked an internal port bind; the unsandboxed build completed successfully.
Pre-existing unrelated line-ending-only changes remain in Design Notes/tech_design.md and docs/v1/module-status.json; they were not modified for this feature.

Coding model used: GPT-5.5 High.
Fix pass for review findings on the previously verified 01-app-shell-home module; status is returned to implementation_done for independent verification.
Added a Home Import Sheet entry that navigates to the Sheet Library route shell and explicitly states that import workflow is not enabled yet.
Clarified the Sheet Library shell copy so import intent lands on a clear empty route shell without claiming real import support.
Made AppShell diagnostics/devtools affordance recoverable: hiding devtools for the session leaves a visible restore entry, and the in-memory state also resets on reload.

handoffNotes: Assigned feature: 02-quick-metronome.
Coding model used: GPT-5.5 High.

Implemented only the Quick Metronome v0 contract scope: tempo, meter, subdivision, accent, countdown, tap tempo, metronome playback, quick recording, replay, quick session/recording metadata, persistence, and a minimal latest quick recording outlet.
Metronome UI calls a BrowserMetronomeService boundary that uses Web Audio scheduling; the UI does not directly call Web Audio scheduler APIs.

Recording UI calls a BrowserRecordingService boundary that uses MediaRecorder; E2E injects a controlled synthetic audio stream through getUserMedia and verifies a non-empty quick recording is saved and replayable.

Code review fix pass: BrowserMetronomeService now emits scheduled tick trace evidence with audioTime, BPM, expected interval, subdivision, and accent status so E2E can measure actual scheduled spacing instead of relying on UI text.

Code review fix pass: BrowserRecordingService now decodes the captured artifact after stop and records duration, sample rate, peak, RMS, estimated frequency, and silence status; E2E independently decodes the saved data URL and verifies the expected 440Hz synthetic input is present without obvious truncation or silence.
QA fix pass: BPM text input now keeps a draft value while typing and commits/clamps only on blur, Enter, stepper, or tap tempo, so values like 60 can be entered normally.
QA fix pass: time signature, subdivision, accent, and countdown are disabled with explanatory text while the metronome is running or counting down; BPM remains live-adjustable.
QA fix pass: empty latest-recording and recordings outlets now show a clearly marked playable demo synthetic 440Hz WAV sample that is not persisted or labeled as a user recording.

Review fix pass on staged QA diff: E2E now decodes the demo WAV data URL with browser AudioContext and verifies duration, RMS, peak, and estimated 440Hz frequency; unit tests also validate the RIFF/WAVE PCM header.
Review fix pass on staged QA diff: E2E now covers BPM blur commit and draft-then-stepper behavior.

Review fix pass on staged QA diff: BrowserMetronomeService now uses a Tone.js adapter boundary with Tone.Transport scheduled callbacks and Tone.Synth triggerAttackRelease instead of hand-written Web Audio oscillator/setInterval scheduling.
Review fix pass on staged QA diff: the Tone adapter stop path clears the scheduled event, stops/cancels Tone.Transport, and disposes the Synth to avoid global transport pollution.
Review fix pass on staged QA diff: unit tests inject a fake Tone adapter to prove scheduled callbacks trigger the Tone instrument path and emit trace evidence with matching scheduled time.
Quick recordings are saved with type quick, linked to a Practice Session with sourceType quick, and have sheetId null.
The /recordings route remains a minimal latest-recording outlet only; full recording library/search/waveform behavior remains for 03-recordings-review.
Implementation commit hash is reported in the final handoff response because a git commit cannot contain its own final object hash without changing that hash.
changedAreas: src/lib/quick-metronome adds pure control helpers, session/recording metadata creation, local persistence, BrowserMetronomeService, BrowserRecordingService, and recording replay service boundaries
src/lib/quick-metronome/metronome-service.ts adds scheduler trace events for timing verification
src/lib/quick-metronome/recording-service.ts and src/lib/quick-metronome/types.ts add decoded artifact analysis metadata
src/lib/quick-metronome/demo-recording.ts adds a real playable synthetic WAV demo recording marked with origin demo
src/lib/quick-metronome/control.ts adds BPM draft commit normalization used by the UI and unit tests
src/lib/quick-metronome/metronome-service.ts now wraps Tone.Transport and Tone.Synth behind the BrowserMetronomeService adapter boundary
src/components/quick-metronome adds the Quick Metronome client experience and latest quick recording outlet
src/components/quick-metronome/quick-metronome-experience.tsx adds BPM draft editing and locks pre-run meter/countdown controls while running
src/components/quick-metronome/latest-quick-recording.tsx shows a clearly marked demo synthetic recording when no saved user recording exists
src/app/quick-metronome/page.tsx now renders the real Quick Metronome workflow
src/app/recordings/page.tsx exposes the minimal latest quick recording outlet for v0 continuity without implementing the full recordings module
src/components/home/home-dashboard.tsx updates Quick Metronome and quick recording copy now that the module is implemented
tests/unit/quick-metronome-control.test.ts and tests/unit/quick-metronome-session.test.ts cover tempo bounds, meter/subdivision/accent/countdown validation, tap tempo, timing, and quick metadata

tests/unit/quick-metronome-metronome-service.test.ts covers Tone transport start/stop/cancel/clear/dispose lifecycle, scheduled callback rescheduling on BPM change, instrument triggerAttackRelease, and trace/trigger time alignment
tests/unit/quick-metronome-recording-analysis.test.ts covers decoded synthetic input frequency/amplitude detection and silent artifact detection

tests/e2e/quick-metronome.spec.ts covers visible controls, BPM draft input behavior including blur, Enter, and draft plus stepper paths, running-state control lockout, tap tempo, measured scheduler trace timing at selected BPM and after BPM change, demo synthetic replay/outlet with browser AudioContext decode, decoded controlled synthetic recording contents, replay, persistence after reload, microphone denial, and recording/metronome independence
tests/e2e/app-shell-home.spec.ts and tests/unit/home-dashboard.test.ts update Home assertions for the implemented quick recording path
knownRisks: Recording format and replay availability depend on browser MediaRecorder and HTMLAudioElement support; unsupported browsers show an explicit recording error.

Quick recording audio is persisted as a data URL in localStorage for the v0 latest-recording outlet; this is suitable for short quick takes but not a long-term storage design for the full recordings module.
The demo synthetic recording is intentionally not persisted and is labeled as demo synthetic/not saved to avoid presenting it as a real user take.
Metronome timing is now self-tested through scheduled Web Audio trace intervals in Chromium; independent verification should still confirm the same tolerance on the target environment.
Playwright's configured webServer uses port 3101 and exited after the E2E run; no separate dev server was left running.

Empty-state demo and synthetic recording outlets were clearly labeled as demo/not saved, replayable, and browser-decodable.

Original quick-metronome contract still passed for timing trace, recording artifact, permission denial, persistence, independence flows, and console/pageerror cleanliness.

handoffNotes: Assigned feature: 03-recordings-review.
Coding model used: GPT-5.5 High.

Implemented only the Recordings / Review v0 contract: unified recordings list, empty state, search, type filter, details, playback, waveform, error markers, delete, Practice Again, and bad/missing audio state.
Recordings UI calls recording history, artifact/waveform, playback, and continue-target services; UI does not directly call IndexedDB/Dexie, raw file APIs, audio element internals, or wavesurfer.js.
Playback/waveform uses wavesurfer.js through RecordingWaveformPlaybackAdapter; artifact validation and simplified peaks are behind loadRecordingArtifactDetails.

Review fix pass: loadRecordingArtifactDetails now always validates and decodes audioDataUrl before returning playback-ready details; trustedPeaks are used only for waveform rendering after the real artifact decodes.
Review fix pass: decoded duration is compared with metadata duration using tolerance and mismatch is surfaced in the details UI as a warning instead of silently accepting stale metadata.

Review fix pass: recordings E2E no longer monkey-patches HTMLMediaElement.play to swallow rejection; it records unhandled playback rejections, verifies none occur, browser-decodes quick and sheet WAV artifacts, and exercises sheet playback.

QA fix pass: Practice Again now remains context-only for quick recordings; saving a later quick take creates a new recording entry and a new session instead of overwriting the source recording artifact.
QA fix pass: quick recording metadata duration now uses decoded artifact duration when available, so newly saved takes align saved duration with the actual playable artifact.
QA fix pass: quick recording persistence defensively assigns a fresh recording id if a caller tries to save a new take with an existing recording id, preserving the original recording and artifact.
Review low-finding fix pass: Practice Again E2E now captures quick-alpha's seeded artifact and immutable metadata before recording again, then asserts exact equality after the later take is saved.

Review low-finding fix pass: Recordings detail action is now labeled Practice Again in UI, contract, and tests to clarify that it returns to practice with source context and must not overwrite the historical recording.

The history repository preserves the existing quick-metronome local data boundary and adds marker-aware delete semantics so quick, sheet, and error marker records can coexist in one persisted snapshot.

E2E seeds real decodable WAV artifacts for quick and sheet recordings, checks decoded waveform peaks for quick, trusted peaks for sheet only after artifact validation, playback event evidence, marker ordering, Practice Again routes, Practice Again followed by another quick recording, delete persistence/artifact inaccessibility, bad audio, trustedPeaks with missing/bad audio, and duration mismatch warning handling.
No recording creation, sheet import/viewer, automatic scoring, export, archive, tags, favorites, or v1 analysis behavior was implemented.
Implementation commit hash is reported in the final handoff response because a git commit cannot contain its own final object hash without changing that hash.
changedAreas: src/app/recordings/page.tsx now renders the real Recordings Review experience instead of the placeholder latest-recording outlet
src/components/recordings-review adds the client UI for empty state, unified list, search/type filtering, details, waveform, playback controls, marker list, delete confirmation, and Practice Again
src/components/recordings-review adds decoded-vs-metadata duration mismatch warning display in recording details

src/lib/recordings-review adds pure history/filter/format helpers, local history repository, audio artifact validation/peak derivation, duration tolerance handling, and wavesurfer playback adapter boundaries
src/lib/quick-metronome/persistence.ts and src/lib/quick-metronome/types.ts preserve marker data and sheet-compatible session metadata when quick recordings are saved
src/lib/quick-metronome/persistence.ts now prevents accidental overwrite when a continued take is saved with an existing recording id
src/lib/quick-metronome/session.ts now stores quick recording duration from decoded artifact analysis when available

tests/unit/recordings-review-history.test.ts covers filtering, sorting, formatting, Practice Again targets, marker ordering, trusted peaks after decode, trustedPeaks with missing/bad audio, derived peaks, missing audio errors, and duration mismatch warnings
tests/unit/recordings-review-repository.test.ts covers local history loading and delete persistence for metadata, artifact access, and linked error markers

tests/e2e/recordings-review.spec.ts covers empty state, seeded real WAV recordings, browser decode duration/energy evidence, search, quick/sheet filters, details, quick and sheet playback without swallowed rejection, waveform evidence, markers, Practice Again routes, using Practice Again from a seeded quick recording then recording again without overwriting the original, delete persistence, artifact removal, bad audio, trustedPeaks with missing/bad audio, and duration mismatch warning
tests/unit/quick-metronome-session.test.ts covers decoded-duration quick metadata and duplicate-id save protection for continued takes
tests/e2e/quick-metronome.spec.ts updates /recordings assertions for the implemented unified review page
knownRisks: The v0 repository continues using the existing localStorage-backed quick recording boundary from 02; large or long recordings remain a future storage concern for Local Data rather than this review UI.
wavesurfer.js is isolated behind an adapter and verified in Chromium E2E; broader browser/audio codec compatibility should be rechecked when additional recording formats are introduced.
Sheet recordings are supported from seeded/persisted metadata for review and continue routing, but sheet recording creation remains out of scope until Sheet Practice recording work exists.
Hydration is guarded so SSR does not read localStorage; users may briefly see the empty shell before local recordings hydrate.

handoffNotes: Assigned feature: 04-sheet-library.
Coding model used: GPT-5.5 High.

Implemented only the Sheet Library contract scope: empty state, real PDF/image import, manual metadata, list display, search, fixed category filtering, Sheet Practice route-shell navigation, delete, unsupported file, bad PDF, and bad image states.

Review fix pass: artifact readability after reload now calls adapter-level PDF.js parsing for PDF artifacts and browser image decoding/dimension checks for image artifacts instead of trusting Blob size.
Review fix pass: E2E captures the actual persisted sheet id and proves metadata and artifact records are both inaccessible after delete.
Review fix pass: Home Import Sheet copy now describes the real Sheet Library import flow instead of saying import is disabled.
UI calls the Sheet Library service boundary and does not call Dexie/IndexedDB, raw storage, PDF.js, or image decoding internals directly.
PDF metadata extraction uses PDF.js through the sheet import adapter; browser image dimensions use browser decoding APIs through the same adapter.
Sheet metadata and file artifacts are persisted in IndexedDB through a Dexie repository boundary, with Blob artifacts stored separately from sheet metadata and deleted together.
E2E imports real fixtures from test-fixtures/sheets rather than creating fake imported rows.
Opening a sheet routes to /sheet-practice?sheetId=... and intentionally leaves the existing Sheet Practice route shell in place; no Sheet Practice viewer behavior was implemented.
Implementation commit hash is reported in the final handoff response because the status commit cannot contain its own final object hash.
changedAreas: src/app/sheet-library/page.tsx now renders the real Sheet Library experience
src/components/sheet-library adds the import form, preview, empty state, list, search/filter controls, route link, and delete UI
src/domain/sheet adds sheet types, metadata validation, search/filter helpers, page count formatting, and Sheet Practice route target generation
src/services/sheet-library adds the service boundary for import, list, artifact status, delete, and repository/adapter contracts
src/infrastructure/files adds the PDF.js/image decode import adapter, real persisted artifact inspection, and Dexie/IndexedDB sheet artifact repository
src/components/home/home-dashboard.tsx updates the Import Sheet entry copy to point to the live Sheet Library import flow
src/types/pdfjs-webpack.d.ts adds typing for the PDF.js bundled worker entry
test-fixtures/sheets adds real PDF/PNG fixtures plus unsupported, bad PDF, and bad image fixtures
tests/unit adds Sheet Library domain, service, adapter-status boundary, and Home copy coverage
tests/e2e/sheet-library.spec.ts adds real browser import, reload artifact parse/decode evidence, persistence, search/filter, route shell, delete metadata/artifact checks, and error-state coverage
tests/e2e/app-shell-home.spec.ts updates Home entry coverage for the live import flow copy
knownRisks: PDF rendering/viewing is not implemented here by contract; this module only parses import metadata and persists the artifact for later Sheet Practice viewer work.

Playwright targeted E2E needed unsandboxed execution when Turbopack internal port binding was blocked by the sandbox; the final full npm run test:e2e passed and port 3101 was confirmed free afterward.
The first full E2E run in this fix pass hit an unrelated existing Quick Metronome synthetic frequency flake; rerunning the full E2E suite passed.
PDF.js uses the package-provided legacy webpack worker entry so browser metadata extraction depends on the bundler supporting module workers, which the production build and E2E validated.

Addressed low review finding: moved artifact object URL creation/revocation behind the SheetViewerService and browser hook boundary; SheetViewerExperience no longer imports or calls browserSheetViewerAdapter directly.

Browser object URLs are now owned by a browser hook/service boundary and revoked on viewer state changes/unmount; long-session memory behavior for very large image sheets can be revisited later.

handoffNotes: Assigned feature: 05e-session-integration.
Coding model used: GPT-5 Codex, no fast tier.

Implemented only the Sheet Practice session integration contract scope: sourceType sheet session creation/restoration, sheetId binding, basic duration, lastPracticedAt updates, recent/Continue Practice target, persistence, and invalid sheet guard.

Added a clearly scoped 05e Sheet Practice session harness because 05b/05c UI controls are not implemented yet; it emits high-level metronome and recording triggers without pretending those submodules are complete.
Recording trigger support creates only sheet recording metadata linked to sessionId and sheetId; it does not create recording audio artifacts or call MediaRecorder.
Metronome and recording trigger states remain independent at the session metadata boundary: each can start or stop without forcing the other state to change.
Sheet Practice UI talks to the practice-session service boundary and does not call Tone.js, MediaRecorder, Dexie, recording internals, or viewer/storage internals directly.
Home Continue Practice now reads the recent practice-session target and routes sheet sessions to /sheet-practice/[sheetId].
Review fix: Continue Practice now validates that the recent sheet session's sheet still exists before returning a Sheet Practice target, so stale sessions after sheet deletion are ignored.

Review fix: 05e sheet recording metadata now writes through the existing Recordings Review history boundary instead of a disconnected Dexie recordings table; the session Dexie schema migrates to sessions-only storage.

Review fix: metadata-only sheet recordings are visible through the shared /recordings history with no audio artifact, preserving the 05e no-audio constraint while giving 05c/03 one canonical metadata path.

Strict review fix: Home Continue Practice now uses a global practice-session repository adapter that includes existing Quick Metronome sessions from the shared recording history snapshot and sheet sessions from Dexie, with recency sorting across both.
Strict review fix: stopping the final active 05e trigger now persists endedAt and duration through an explicit endPracticeSession service operation.
Strict review fix: PracticeSession and SheetRecordingMetadata now have Zod schemas applied at save/load/metadata bridge boundaries to reject invalid sourceType/sheetId/date/duration combinations.

Strict review fix: Sheet Practice route construction is centralized in domain/sheet routes and reused by Home Continue, Sheet Library, and Recordings Review while preserving query route compatibility for recordingId links.
Strict review fix: Sheet Practice harness duration display now reuses the shared practice duration formatter instead of a local formatter.

Strict re-review fix: practice date validation now uses Zod's z.iso.datetime({ offset: true }) with a strict round-trip refinement for real ISO instants, covering non-ISO strings, impossible dates, and invalid timezone/format cases.
Strict re-review fix: the recording history metadata bridge validates PracticeSession before saving and enforces sheet sourceType plus matching recording/session and recording/sheet linkage.
Implementation commit hash is reported in the final handoff response because this status commit cannot contain its own final object hash.
changedAreas: src/domain/practice adds sheet/quick practice session, sheet recording metadata, Continue Practice target, duration, and independent trigger rules

src/services/practice-session adds the service/repository boundary for sheet session creation/restoration, duration updates, recording metadata linking, recent session, Continue Practice target, and clear/subscribe
src/infrastructure/db adds the Dexie-backed browser practice-session repository and browser service composition
src/services/sheet-library and src/infrastructure/files add a bounded lastPracticedAt update API used by 05e
src/components/sheet-practice/session adds the minimal 05e session trigger harness for contract verification before 05b/05c UI exists
src/components/sheet-practice/viewer/sheet-viewer-experience.tsx composes the 05e harness only for a valid loaded sheet
src/hooks/use-practice-session-dashboard.ts and src/components/home/home-dashboard.tsx wire the recent Continue Practice target
src/infrastructure/db/recording-history-metadata-repository.ts bridges 05e sheet recording metadata into the existing recordings-review local history repository
src/infrastructure/db/global-practice-session-repository.ts adapts existing quick sessions and sheet sessions into one recent-session calculation
src/infrastructure/db/practice-session-repository.ts removes the isolated recordings object store through a Dexie v2 sessions-only migration
src/domain/practice/validation.ts adds Zod validation for practice sessions and sheet recording metadata
src/infrastructure/db/recording-history-metadata-repository.ts now validates bridge sessions and rejects mismatched sheet recording linkage before writing shared history
src/domain/sheet/routes.ts centralizes canonical Sheet Practice path and query route construction
src/domain/practice/format.ts provides shared practice duration formatting
tests/unit/practice-session-service.test.ts covers 05e session rules and trigger independence
tests/unit/recordings-review-repository.test.ts covers 05e sheet metadata visibility and bridge linkage rejection through the shared recording history boundary

tests/e2e/sheet-practice-session.spec.ts covers real browser sheet import, no-session-on-view, metronome-only, recording metadata, independence, reload persistence, Continue Practice, last practiced, and unknown sheet guard
knownRisks: The Sheet Practice controls are intentionally a minimal 05e harness until 05b practice controls and 05c sheet recording UI are implemented; final parent integration must repeat these checks through real controls.
The 05e recording path persists metadata only and deliberately has no audio artifact, waveform, replay, or MediaRecorder behavior.
Practice duration is a basic elapsed-time calculation from session start to the latest trigger/update, not a precise play/pause event timeline.
Home summary uses the currently loaded recent session for 05e continuity and is not a full 08-practice-session history aggregation implementation.
Metadata-only sheet recordings appear in Recordings Review but cannot play until 05c supplies real recording artifacts; the current artifact panel correctly reports the missing audio artifact.
Existing clients with the initial 05e Dexie recordings object store migrate to a sessions-only practice-session DB; new sheet metadata is canonicalized through the Recordings Review history boundary.
A previous strict-review run saw an unrelated 02 Quick Metronome frequency threshold flake; the strict re-review full E2E rerun passed all 9 chromium tests.
The local port cleanup check needed unsandboxed execution because sandboxed Node port binding returned EPERM; the unsandboxed one-shot bind check confirmed port 3101 was free.

acceptanceCriteria: Opening a valid sheet does not create a session until metronome or recording activity begins.
Metronome trigger creates or restores a sheet session with sourceType sheet and the active sheetId.
Metronome-only activity does not create a recording.
Recording trigger creates or restores a sheet session and links sheet recording metadata to sessionId and sheetId.
Metronome and recording remain independent when starting or stopping either trigger.
Stopping the last active trigger persists endedAt and duration.
Continue Practice is global across quick and sheet sessions, and recency wins across both.
A stale sheet session whose sheet was deleted is ignored by Continue Practice.
Sheet lastPracticed updates after real activity and persists after reload.
Session metadata persists after reload.
Zod validation rejects invalid persisted session and sheet recording metadata dates, durations, sourceType, and sheet linkage.
The recording history bridge validates session and recording linkage before writing shared history.
Sheet recording metadata is visible through the shared Recordings Review boundary without claiming audio playback or artifact UI.
Missing or unknown sheet does not create an invalid session.
No 05b or 05c UI/audio behavior is claimed complete by this verification.
e2eEvidence: tests/e2e/sheet-practice-session.spec.ts passed in the full Playwright run and covered valid sheet open with no session until activity, metronome-triggered session creation, recording-triggered session creation, metronome/recording independence, reload persistence, Continue Practice routing, lastPracticed updates, shared recordings history visibility, stale sheet deletion handling, unknown sheet guard, and console/pageerror capture.

handoffNotes: Assigned feature: 05b-practice-controls.
Coding model used: GPT-5.5 High, no fast tier.

Implemented only the Sheet Practice controls contract: bottom practice controls, metronome play/stop, BPM/time signature/subdivision/accent/countdown settings, visible playing/stopped state, sheet default initialization, session activity trigger, and responsive desktop/mobile layout.
Reused the verified 02 Quick Metronome BrowserMetronomeService, Tone adapter boundary, settings helpers, and scheduled tick trace event; no new scheduler, oscillator, or metronome service was added.
Reused the verified 05e browserPracticeSessionService ensureSheetSession/update/end APIs for sheet session activity; no parallel session path was added.
Starting the Sheet Practice metronome creates or restores a sheet session and updates sheet practice activity, but does not create recording artifacts or recording metadata.
Recording independence is covered through a small recording-active harness state inside the controls; it does not call MediaRecorder, Dexie recording writes, or sheet recording metadata creation.
Sheet Practice UI continues to stay behind service/hook boundaries and does not directly call Tone.js, Web Audio scheduler internals, MediaRecorder, Dexie, PDF internals, or image decoding internals.
Updated the previous 05e E2E browser path to use real 05b controls instead of the temporary session metronome trigger harness.

Strict review fix: Sheet Practice now ensures/restores the sheet session before starting Tone playback and still calls metronome stop on every failure path; unit coverage injects a rejecting session service and proves Tone start is not called and stop cleanup runs.

Strict review fix: visible fake Rec On/Rec Off production buttons were removed. Recording independence is now driven only by a test-injected harness event, with no recording UI/audio/metadata implemented for 05b.
Strict review fix: unsupported sheet meters such as 5/4 now show an explicit recoverable fallback message instead of silently discarding sheet metadata.
Strict review fix: browser timing coverage now selects Every beat and Off accents and verifies the changed accent trace patterns.
Strict review fix: shared BPM draft commit/step behavior was extracted into useMetronomeBpmDraft and reused by Quick Metronome and Sheet Practice controls.
Second strict review fix: shared transport/countdown/start/stop/update/cleanup behavior was extracted into useMetronomeTransport and reused by Quick Metronome and Sheet Practice controls.

Second strict review fix: if a sheet session is ensured but Tone startup rejects, Sheet Practice stops playback state and ends only metronome-only sessions; recording-linked sessions are deliberately left intact.

Third strict review fix: Sheet Practice metronome start now records explicit ownership by checking for an existing sheet session before ensureSheetSession; Tone-start failure only rolls back a session created by this metronome start, so existing sessions and active recording sessions without saved metadata are preserved.

Third strict review fix: Sheet Practice now locks meter, subdivision, countdown, and accent while running or counting down, while leaving BPM editable; browser coverage exercises both running and countdown lock states.
Implementation commit hash is reported in the final handoff response because this status commit cannot contain its own final object hash.
changedAreas: src/components/sheet-practice/controls adds SheetPracticeControls and default initialization helper for BPM/time signature fallback
src/lib/quick-metronome/use-bpm-draft.ts adds a shared BPM draft hook reused by Quick Metronome and Sheet Practice controls
src/lib/quick-metronome/use-metronome-transport.ts adds shared transport, countdown, start/stop, update, cleanup, and pre-start/failure hook behavior
src/services/practice-session exposes getRecentSheetSession through the existing 05e service boundary so 05b can determine metronome-start ownership without creating a parallel session path
src/components/quick-metronome/quick-metronome-experience.tsx now reuses the shared BPM draft hook
src/components/quick-metronome/quick-metronome-experience.tsx now reuses the shared metronome transport hook while keeping quick recording messaging local

src/components/sheet-practice/viewer/sheet-viewer-experience.tsx composes the bottom controls for valid loaded sheets and removes the temporary visible 05e metronome trigger harness from the Sheet Practice workspace
tests/unit/quick-metronome-transport.test.tsx covers shared metronome transport start/stop, countdown, pre-start context, and failure cleanup

tests/unit/sheet-practice-controls.test.tsx covers sheet default initialization, explicit unsupported meter fallback, ownership-based session failure cleanup including Tone-start rejection after session ensure, non-owned existing sessions, active recording sessions without saved metadata, and shared metronome trace accent/subdivision evidence

tests/e2e/sheet-practice-controls.spec.ts covers 72 BPM sheet defaults, real metronome play/stop, BPM change timing, running/countdown lock behavior with BPM still editable, Downbeat/Every beat/Off accent patterns, subdivision density, session trigger, metronome-only no recording, injected recording independence harness, stop trace cleanup, desktop/mobile resize, and console errors
tests/e2e/sheet-practice-session.spec.ts now exercises 05e session persistence and injected independence harness behavior through real 05b controls instead of fake visible trigger buttons
knownRisks: The recording-active state is intentionally test-injected harness state only; production UI does not expose recording controls until 05c implements real sheet recording UI/audio/metadata.
The old 05e SheetPracticeSessionHarness component remains in the tree for historical contract context but is no longer composed in the active Sheet Practice viewer.
The v0 metronome supports only 2/4, 3/4, 4/4, and 6/8; other imported sheet meters show an explicit fallback to 4/4 until the shared metronome contract supports more meters.
Mobile layout keeps controls usable through page scrolling; the full detailed controls panel is taller than a narrow viewport by design rather than compressing all settings into one screen.
Port cleanup was confirmed with an unsandboxed one-shot bind check because sandboxed Node port binding returned EPERM.
